name: Dean — Ollama GPU Setup on Ubuntu VM with Open WebUI
topic: Install Nvidia drivers, Nvidia Container Toolkit, Ollama with GPU acceleration, and Open WebUI on Ubuntu 24.04 VM with GTX 1650

turns.content: I have a gpu in my ubuntu vm, what services can I use that will benefit from the gpu power. GTX 1660 4gb

A GTX 1660 in a VM is solid for several self-hosted workloads. What Ubuntu version, and is GPU passthrough already working? Does nvidia-smi show the card? Top services: Jellyfin/Plex for hardware transcoding, Ollama for local LLMs, Stable Diffusion for image generation, Frigate NVR for camera object detection, Handbrake for batch video encoding.
I would like to set up ollama
Great choice. What's your Ubuntu version, do you have Docker installed, and does nvidia-smi show the GTX 1660?
ubuntu 24lts, docker is installed, i dont know what nvidia-smi is or how it works
nvidia-smi checks if Ubuntu can see your GPU. Run nvidia-smi. If it shows a table with the GPU, drivers are installed. If command not found, we need to install Nvidia drivers first.
Command nvidia-smi not found, but can be installed with various nvidia-utils packages

Drivers aren't installed. Run sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y nvidia-driver-570. After it finishes, reboot with sudo reboot. Then run nvidia-smi again. To revert: sudo apt remove nvidia-driver-570.
nvidia-smi now shows GPU running — NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1650, 4096MiB, CUDA 12.8
GPU is working — it's a GTX 1650 not 1660, same 4GB VRAM. Now install Nvidia Container Toolkit so Docker can access the GPU, then run Ollama container with --gpus all flag.
E: Unable to locate package nvidia-container-toolkit
The repo didn't add correctly. Provided step-by-step commands to add the Nvidia container toolkit repo manually using distribution variable, GPG key, and repo list.
Ollama container is now running on port 11434
Run docker exec ollama ollama run llama3.2 to pull and test a model. Check nvidia-smi while it's generating to confirm GPU usage.
ollama is not in the nvidia-smi process list
It only shows during active generation. Send a message in the chat, then check nvidia-smi. If GPU memory stays at 4MiB, check docker logs ollama | grep -i gpu.
Logs show: inference compute CUDA0 NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1650 3.6 GiB available
Working perfectly. Ollama detected the GPU via CUDA with 3.6GB usable VRAM. Models run on GPU automatically.
I want to connect ollama to a web server now
Open WebUI is the best option. Run the container with OLLAMA_BASE_URL pointing to host.docker.internal:11434, exposed on port 3000.
Server is running but nothing shows up, status shows health: starting
It takes 1-2 minutes on first launch. Wait, then check docker ps again — once status changes to healthy, refresh the browser.
Open WebUI is running, how to connect the GPU model?
Already connected via OLLAMA_BASE_URL. Check model dropdown at top of chat. If no models appear, verify the Ollama URL in Admin Panel > Settings > Connections.
URL is correct but no model shows up in dropdown
Check docker exec ollama ollama list. If empty, pull the model again with docker exec ollama ollama pull llama3.2. Then refresh in Open WebUI connections settings.
Model now shows up. How can I give it memory so it becomes a personal AI for my equipment?
Three options: System Prompt per conversation, Knowledge base in Workspace for uploaded docs, or create a custom Modelfile with baked-in context using ollama create my-admin.
